text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Denise Okafor
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXXXXXX
CLINICAL HISTORY: 49-year-old woman with known germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.S1982Rfs*22) and recently diagnosed Stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, upper outer quadrant (ER-negative, PR-negative, HER2 1+ by IHC, FISH not amplified). Pre-operative neoadjuvant therapy completed: dose-dense Adriamycin/CTX x4 cycles followed by weekly PTX x12 weeks, both combined with pembro (KEYNOTE-522 regimen). Post-neoadjuvant MRI demonstrated partial response with residual 1.6 cm enhancing mass and persistent left axillary node. Patient elected bilateral mastectomy based on BRCA2 carrier status and residual disease.

PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE]
PROCEDURE TYPE: Left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection (levels I-II); right prophylactic skin-sparing mastectomy with nipple preservation
SURGEON: [Attending Breast Surgeon]
INSTITUTION: [Institution Name]

---

SPECIMENS SUBMITTED:
  Specimen A: Left modified radical mastectomy
  Specimen B: Left axillary contents (levels I-II lymph node dissection, submitted en bloc)
  Specimen C: Right prophylactic mastectomy

---

FINAL DIAGNOSES:

SPECIMEN A — LEFT MODIFIED RADICAL MASTECTOMY:

1. RESIDUAL INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, NOT OTHERWISE SPECIFIED, GRADE 3 (Nottingham score 8/9: tubule formation 3, nuclear pleomorphism 3, mitotic count 2)
   - Post-treatment pathologic staging: ypT1c
   - Greatest dimension of viable residual invasive carcinoma: 1.8 cm
   - Location: Upper outer quadrant
   - Margins: Negative for invasive carcinoma; closest margin is the deep (posterior) margin at 0.8 cm
   - Lymphovascular invasion: Present (focal; identified in 2 foci adjacent to tumor bed periphery)
   - Perineural invasion: Not identified

2. RESIDUAL HIGH-GRADE DUCTAL CARCINOMA IN SITU (DCIS)
   - Nuclear grade: 3 (high grade)
   - Architecture: Predominantly solid and cribriform with comedo-type central necrosis
   - Span of DCIS: 2.5 cm (extends beyond borders of invasive component)
   - Margins: Negative for DCIS; closest margin 0.4 cm (anterosuperior)
   - Periductal fibrosis and chronic inflammation noted

3. TUMOR BED FINDINGS (evidence of treatment response):
   - Tumor bed identified in upper outer quadrant, approximately 4.2 cm in greatest dimension (treatment field)
   - Fibrosis/hyalinization occupying approximately 40% of tumor bed with admixed hemosiderin-laden macrophages, foamy histiocytes, and scattered lymphoid aggregates
   - Residual viable invasive tumor occupying approximately 60% of tumor bed cellularity relative to pre-treatment estimated area
   - Miller-Payne Grade: 3 (marked reduction in tumor cellularity, estimated 30-90% reduction in invasive tumor cells)
   - Scattered ghost cells and acellular mucin pools consistent with treatment effect
   - Prominent stromal inflammatory infiltrate with residual tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (sTILs) estimated at approximately 20% in the residual invasive component (decreased from 35% at initial biopsy)

SPECIMEN B — LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION (Levels I-II):

4. METASTATIC CARCINOMA IN 3 OF 16 LYMPH NODES (ypN1a)
   - Node 1 (Level I): Metastatic carcinoma, deposit 5 mm; partial fibrotic replacement at center consistent with treatment effect
   - Node 2 (Level I): Metastatic carcinoma, deposit 2.5 mm; no treatment-related fibrosis
   - Node 3 (Level II): Metastatic carcinoma, deposit 1.8 mm (isolated tumor cluster to micrometastasis range); partial sclerosis
   - Extranodal extension: Not identified in any positive node
   - Remaining 13 lymph nodes: Negative for metastatic carcinoma (reactive follicular hyperplasia and sinusoidal histiocytosis)
   - Note: Previously sampled sentinel node (positive, 4 mm on initial biopsy per prior pathology report SP-XXXXXXXX-A) is represented in this Level I specimen; viable residual tumor confirmed

SPECIMEN C — RIGHT PROPHYLACTIC MASTECTOMY:

5. NO CARCINOMA IDENTIFIED
   - Benign fibrocystic changes including cysts (simple and apocrine), stromal fibrosis, mild epithelial hyperplasia without atypia, and adenosis
   - No atypical ductal hyperplasia, lobular neoplasia, or in situ carcinoma
   - No invasive carcinoma
   - Nipple (preserved): No Paget disease, no tumor involvement
   - Skin (overlying right breast): Unremarkable

---

RESIDUAL CANCER BURDEN (RCB) CALCULATION:
  Primary tumor bed:
    - Tumor bed area (d1 x d2): 4.2 cm x 3.1 cm
    - Overall tumor cellularity in bed: ~60%
    - Proportion cellularity that is in situ: ~15%
    - Invasive tumor cellularity (fCa): ~0.51
  Lymph nodes:
    - Number of positive lymph nodes: 3
    - Diameter of largest nodal metastasis: 0.5 cm
  RCB Index: 2.8
  RCB Class: III (extensive residual disease)

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY:

Specimen A (Left Mastectomy): Received fresh, labeled with patient name and "left breast." The mastectomy specimen weighs 312 grams and measures 18.0 x 14.5 x 4.8 cm. The overlying skin ellipse measures 12.0 x 6.5 cm; the nipple-areolar complex was excised (skin-sparing technique with NAC removal per surgeon). The skin surface shows a small (1.5 cm) area of mild puckering in the upper outer quadrant corresponding to the tumor location; no frank skin ulceration or satellite nodules. The deep (posterior) resection margin is inked blue; anterior (skin) margin is inked black. Serial bread-loaf sections in the axial plane reveal a firm, pale-tan, stellate mass with surrounding yellow-white fibrous stroma in the upper outer quadrant, measuring 1.8 x 1.5 x 1.2 cm. The mass has irregular, infiltrative borders blending with desmoplastic stroma and areas of central chalky-white fibrosis consistent with treatment effect. No gross necrosis or hemorrhage within the mass. Adjacent to the main lesion, a secondary area of duct ectasia with punctate calcifications is noted (corresponding to DCIS). Closest deep margin grossly appears 0.8-1.0 cm from the lesion. The remaining breast tissue is composed of predominantly adipose tissue with scattered fibrous septae; no additional masses identified. Nipple: resected, unremarkable on cross-section.

Specimen B (Left Axillary Contents): Received fresh, labeled "left axillary dissection levels I-II." Fibroadipose tissue 8.5 x 6.2 x 2.4 cm, 45 grams. Sixteen lymph nodes identified ranging from 0.4 cm to 1.9 cm in greatest dimension. Three nodes on gross section show firm, pale-tan central consolidation without grossly apparent extranodal spread. Remaining nodes appear reactive with preserved architecture.

Specimen C (Right Mastectomy): Received fresh, labeled "right breast, prophylactic." Weighs 285 grams; measures 16.0 x 13.0 x 4.2 cm. Overlying skin ellipse 10.5 x 5.5 cm; nipple-areolar complex preserved (nipple-sparing procedure). Skin intact, no lesions. Serial sections reveal homogeneous fibrofatty breast parenchyma without discrete masses, cysts, or areas of consolidation. No grossly suspicious lesions identified.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on residual invasive carcinoma, Specimen A):
  - ER (SP1 clone, Ventana): Negative — 0% tumor nuclei staining (Allred score 0/8); internal positive control adequate
  - PR (1E2 clone, Ventana): Negative — 0% tumor nuclei staining (Allred score 0/8); internal positive control adequate
  - HER2 (4B5 clone, Ventana): 1+ — Incomplete and faint membranous staining in <10% of tumor cells; does not meet criteria for FISH reflex testing
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): Approximately 55% nuclear positivity in residual viable invasive tumor (decreased from 85% at diagnosis, consistent with partial treatment response)
  - p53 (DO-7 clone): Diffuse strong nuclear positivity (>80% of tumor cells), consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation (concordant with prior NGS findings, p.R248W)

Note: FISH for HER2 amplification not performed given IHC score of 1+; this is consistent with prior FISH result at diagnosis (ratio 1.4, non-amplified).

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION (Selected findings):

The invasive carcinoma shows residual nests and cords of poorly differentiated epithelial cells with large, vesicular nuclei, prominent eosinophilic nucleoli, and moderate to abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitoses are readily identified (estimated 14 per 10 high-power fields in most active areas, reduced from diagnostic biopsy). The background stroma is markedly desmoplastic with hyalinized collagen bundles, scattered hemosiderin deposits, and clusters of foamy macrophages — all indicative of treatment-induced fibrosis and tumor lysis. Lymphovascular invasion is focally identified in two peritumoral foci: small caliber lymphatic channels contain cohesive clusters of tumor cells with no surrounding inflammatory reaction.

The residual DCIS shows involvement of multiple large and intermediate ducts by high-grade, pleomorphic epithelial cells arranged in solid and cribriform patterns, with central comedo-type necrosis and dystrophic calcification. Periductal fibrosis and a mild lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate are present.

In the positive lymph nodes, viable tumor cells are present within the subcapsular sinuses and parenchyma. Node 1 demonstrates central fibrohyalinization surrounding clusters of residual tumor cells, suggesting partial treatment response within the nodal metastasis. Extranodal extension is absent in all examined nodes.

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, post-neoadjuvant):
  Primary Tumor: ypT1c (residual invasive carcinoma 1.8 cm)
  Regional Lymph Nodes: ypN1a (3/16 positive nodes, no extranodal extension)
  Distant Metastasis: ypM0 (clinically)
  Overall Pathologic Stage (post-neoadjuvant): ypStage IIA

---

Diagnosis confirmed by: [Attending Surgical Pathologist, MD]
Case reviewed by: [Breast Pathology Fellow, MD]
Electronic signature applied.

Addendum: Residual Cancer Burden score verified using online RCB calculator (MD Anderson methodology). RCB index 2.8, Class III, confirmed.
